title: Cutover and verify VPS deployment
description:
Final flip + smoke-test once the upstream beads (api port, sqlite, dockerfile, web url, mafia url, compose) are all merged.

Scope:
- Tag a new release; let docker-publish.yml build both images.
- `ssh hzr "cd ~/pocket-wishlist && docker compose pull && docker compose up -d"`.
- Trigger a price refresh so `/get-prices` and `/lowest-mall` have data (hit `POST /update-prices` and `POST /update-pricegun` with the shared secret, or wait for whatever cron drives them).
- Run an end-to-end check from a real KoLmafia install: have the new wishlist-mafia script POST `/update-wishlist`, then verify the web UI on the VPS reads it back correctly.
- Verify CORS, TLS, and that 304/ETag/gzip responses from the existing server.ts code path still work behind Caddy.
- DNS flip (if applicable) — point the production domain at the VPS.

Acceptance:
- Web UI loads at production domain, shows prices, shows a freshly-synced wishlist.
- `wishlist-mafia` round trip: script push → API write → web read works.
- No CORS errors in browser devtools.
- Val Town deployment + GH Pages deploy script remain functional as legacy fallback (don't break them in the cutover).

title: docker-compose + Caddy on VPS (with API CORS)
description:
Stand up the production stack on `ssh hzr`: api + web containers behind Caddy with auto-TLS.

Scope:
- compose.yaml on the VPS with three services:
  - `api` — `ghcr.io/<owner>/pocket-wishlist-api`, env: `SECRET=...`, `DB_PATH=/data/wishlist.db`. Volume `api_data:/data`.
  - `web` — `ghcr.io/<owner>/pocket-wishlist`. Reads its own `VITE_API_BASE` at image-build time, no runtime config needed.
  - `caddy` — official image. Mounts a `Caddyfile` and a volume for cert storage.
- Caddyfile routes:
  - `<web-domain>` → web:3000
  - `<api-domain>` (or `<web-domain>/api/*`) → api:<port>
- Decide between subdomain (`api.<domain>`) and path-prefix (`<domain>/api`); subdomain is simpler for CORS but path-prefix avoids cross-origin entirely. Recommend subdomain.
- Add CORS middleware to wishlist-api's Hono app for the web origin (if subdomain). hono/cors makes this a one-liner.
- Document a deploy command: `ssh hzr "cd ~/pocket-wishlist && docker compose pull && docker compose up -d"`.
- Secrets live in a `.env` next to compose.yaml on the VPS (gitignored, hand-rolled).

Acceptance:
- `https://<web-domain>` serves the SSR app with valid TLS.
- `https://<api-domain>/get-prices` returns JSON (or 500 if regen hasn't happened yet — error surface is fine).
- `docker compose down && up -d` preserves the SQLite DB.

title: Bake new API URL into wishlist-mafia build (hard cutover)
description:
The published KoLmafia script has the val.run URL hardcoded at packages/wishlist-mafia/src/wishlist.ts:186. Replace with a build-time constant and ship a new release.

Scope:
- Replace the literal val.run URL with a constant injected at build time (esbuild `--define`, similar to how the web pipeline will work).
- Default the constant to the new VPS hostname.
- Bump the script version so users can see they're on the new build.
- Update wikilinks/install instructions if any reference the version.

Hard-cutover consequences (already accepted in the epic):
- Users running an older `dist/wishlist.js` will keep POSTing to val.run. Since the val is staying up as a legacy fallback (per the epic), their writes will go to the val.town SQLite-less blob storage, not the new VPS DB. They will appear "stuck on the old data" until they update the script. This is fine.

Acceptance:
- `dist/wishlist.js` from a fresh `bun run build` contains the new VPS hostname, not val.run.
- A release-branch publish via the existing build-script.yml workflow ships the new build to users on next pull.

title: Centralize API base URL in wishlist-web (replace hardcoded val.run references)
description:
Three call sites bake the val.run hostname directly into the bundled JS. Replace them with a single config point so production builds hit the new VPS API and dev/preview can hit anything.

Sites:
- packages/wishlist-web/src/api/mall.ts:12
- packages/wishlist-web/src/api/wishlist.ts:18
- packages/wishlist-web/src/api/wishlist.ts:51

Scope:
- Introduce `VITE_API_BASE` (or equivalent build-time constant) read from env at build time.
- Helper module (e.g. src/api/_base.ts) that exports the constant; the three call sites import from it and template URLs.
- Set the prod value in the docker-compose build step / GH Actions image build for the new VPS host.
- Local dev defaults to either val.run or `http://localhost:3001` — pick one and document.

Note: The existing GH Pages `bun run deploy` path stays in place as legacy fallback per the epic. It can keep using val.run by setting `VITE_API_BASE` accordingly in that build path — no need to break it.

Acceptance:
- No literal "resprit--..." or ".val.run" strings remain in packages/wishlist-web/src.
- `grep -r "val.run" packages/wishlist-web/src` returns nothing.
- Built bundle for the VPS image points at the VPS API host; legacy GH Pages build still works against val.run.

title: Add Dockerfile + ghcr publish workflow for wishlist-api
description:
Mirror the existing wishlist-web container pattern (root Dockerfile + .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml) for wishlist-api.

Scope:
- Add a Dockerfile (either at packages/wishlist-api/Dockerfile or a second root Dockerfile.api) that:
  - Uses oven/bun:latest.
  - Copies root package.json, packages/wishlist-shared, packages/wishlist-api.
  - Runs bun install at the workspace root.
  - CMD `bun run start` (or `bun src/index.ts`) from the api workdir.
  - EXPOSEs the API port.
- Either (a) generalize docker-publish.yml to build/publish two images keyed by Dockerfile, or (b) add a sibling api-docker-publish.yml. Same trigger (`v*` tags) and same ghcr registry pattern.
- Image name: `ghcr.io/<owner>/pocket-wishlist-api` (or similar — match repo conventions).

Acceptance:
- `docker build -f <api-dockerfile> .` produces a runnable image.
- Tagging `vX.Y.Z` on main publishes both web and api images to ghcr.io.

title: Replace Val Town blob storage with SQLite (bun:sqlite)
description:
Swap every `blob.getJSON` / `blob.setJSON` call in wishlist-api for a SQLite-backed store using `bun:sqlite`.

Scope:
- Schema sketch:
  - `wishlists(user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, username TEXT, last_updated INTEGER, wishlist_json TEXT)` — replaces `wish/<userId>` blob keys.
  - `kv(key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value_json TEXT, updated_at INTEGER)` — replaces `lowestMall`, `pricegun`, `prices`, `pricesLastUpdate` blob keys (all four are single-record JSON values).
- Thin storage module (e.g. `src/db.ts`) with typed get/set helpers; routes call into it instead of importing `blob` directly.
- DB file path comes from env (e.g. `DB_PATH=/data/wishlist.db`) so compose can mount a volume to it.
- Run `db.exec(...)` migrations at startup.
- No data migration from val.town — regenerate from scratch (mall data refills on next pricegun update; wishlists refill as users sync from KoLmafia).

Acceptance:
- All 11 `blob.*` call sites in src/mall.ts and src/wishlist.ts replaced.
- Fresh DB starts empty; endpoints behave correctly across the empty → populated transition.
- DB file persists across container restarts when `/data` is a mounted volume.

title: Port wishlist-api from Deno/Val Town to Bun
description:
Strip Val-Town-specific machinery from packages/wishlist-api so the Hono app runs under Bun.

Scope:
- Replace `import { blob } from "https://esm.town/v/std/blob"` and the `npm:`/`jsr:` specifiers in src/index.ts, src/mall.ts, src/wishlist.ts with bare imports + the Bun-installed packages.
- `Deno.env.get("secret")` → `process.env.SECRET` (or similar env name).
- Delete packages/wishlist-api/schemas/api.ts and import the same schemas from `wishlist-shared` instead — resolves the existing `// TODO: Copy from wishlist-shared` note.
- Remove `deno.json`, `deno.lock`, the `vt watch`/`vt push` scripts, and the `deno` devDep.
- Add `dev` (e.g. `bun run --watch src/index.ts`) and `start` scripts. Default port 3001 (or whatever the compose stack uses).
- Storage layer can stay in-place stubbed against the old `blob` API in this issue — replacement happens in the SQLite bead.

Acceptance:
- `bun install && bun run dev` boots the API locally and `GET /` returns "howdy!".
- No remaining `Deno.`, `https://`, `jsr:`, or `npm:` import specifiers in src/.
- packages/wishlist-api/schemas/api.ts deleted; routes import from `wishlist-shared`.

title: Migrate API off Val Town and frontend off GitHub Pages to self-hosted VPS
description:
Migrate wishlist-api from Val Town (Deno + esm.town blob storage) to a Bun container on the existing self-hosted VPS (`ssh hzr`), and migrate wishlist-web from GitHub Pages to the same VPS using the existing Dockerfile + SSR Bun server. Both services land behind a Caddy reverse proxy with auto-TLS.

Decisions (locked in):
- Two containers + Caddy reverse proxy.
- SQLite via bun:sqlite for storage (single file on disk, mounted as compose volume).
- Hard cutover for the published KoLmafia script — old installs will hit a dead URL until users pull the new release. Documented, accepted.
- VPS already provisioned (Docker present, domain + DNS in place).
- Skip explicit data migration — regenerate `lowestMall`/`pricegun`/`prices` from sources and let `wish/<userId>` data repopulate as users sync.
- Keep Val Town deployment + GH Pages `deploy` script in place as a legacy fallback / alternate build pipeline; do not strip them.

Non-goals (potential follow-up beads, not blockers):
- Replace shared-secret `?auth=` auth.
- Replace user-ID-as-identity in /get-wishlist and /toggle-wishlist (the existing TODOs in src/wishlist.ts).

title: Refactor wishlist virtualization to use pretext for deterministic height computation
description:
Replace DOM-measurement-based layout precomputation in wishlist-web with programmatic height computation via @chenglou/pretext.

## Current state
- packages/wishlist-web/src/components/List.tsx uses @tanstack/react-virtual's useWindowVirtualizer
- packages/wishlist-web/src/hooks/useEntryHeights.ts: heights live in Map<string,number>, populated by a useLayoutEffect that reads clientHeight on every item rendered invisibly in MeasurementContainer (src/components/MeasurementContainer.tsx) at mount
- Loading-time cost: full DOM layout pass over ~1000 items before virtualization can run
- Variable height driven by text wrapping (item name + type label) across Tailwind breakpoints; rest of entry is fixed chrome (image, price column, tier chips)
- Resize across xs/lg breakpoints invalidates the entire cache (100ms debounced) and re-measures

## Proposed approach
Pin @chenglou/pretext@0.0.7 (pre-1.0, ~6wk old, single maintainer — calculated bet). Use prepare() once per item text, then layout(prepared, width, lineHeight) cheaply per viewport width. Model each entry as: fixed_chrome_px + sum(text_block_height(width)) — pretext for the wrapping text, constants for image/padding/chrome.

Headings (84px) and subheadings (32px) stay as constants — no pretext needed.

## Decisions captured
- Goal: full determinism. Eliminate DOM measurement, not just speed it up.
- Risk handling: pin and proceed. No abstraction layer yet (revisit if breakage hits).
- MeasurementContainer removal: decide after a spike validates pretext-computed heights match current DOM heights to within ~1px across xs/sm/md/lg.

## Open questions before implementation
- What's the actual font stack? (pretext warns system-ui is unsafe on macOS)
- How are Tailwind breakpoint widths resolved at runtime — read computed CSS, or hardcode per-breakpoint pixel values?
- Font loading: gate prepare() on document.fonts.ready, or measure with fallback then re-layout?
- Pretext is no-SSR; current code has ClientOnly wrappers — confirm no SSR path needs heights.

## References
- Pretext API: prepare(text, font, opts) -> opaque; layout(prepared, maxWidth, lineHeight) -> {height, lineCount}
- Repo: github.com/chenglou/pretext
notes:
## Phase 1 complete (commit pending)

packages/wishlist-web/src/lib/entryGeometry.ts — pure module mirroring tailwind-clamp's CSS output for every layout primitive in the entry path. Validated against live DOM via Chrome DevTools MCP at xs/sm/md/lg/xl viewports. maxAbsDiff ≤ 0.003px (subpixel rounding only) on rowOuterWidth, sectionContentWidth (xs/sm/md only), imageBoxWidth, nameColumnWidth, nameFontSizePx, nameLineHeightPx, rowPaddingX.

Critical fix during Phase 1: CSS `vw` resolves against the **layout viewport** (`document.documentElement.clientWidth`), not `window.innerWidth`. With a vertical scrollbar present the two differ by ~15px, and feeding innerWidth into clamp formulas gives wrong values near max-padding regions. The module's public API takes layout viewport width as input.

## Phase 2 complete (commit pending)

@chenglou/pretext@0.0.7 installed and integrated via packages/wishlist-web/src/lib/entryNameHeight.ts. nameTextHeight(name, vp) calls pretext.layout() with widths/fonts/line-heights from entryGeometry. Validated against DOM at every breakpoint — pixel-perfect on every viewport-visible name, **including names that wrap to multiple lines**. text-balance did not cause drift on the sample tested (pretext's greedy wrap and the rendered text-balance landed on the same line count).

Caveat: only viewport-visible entries (5–7 per scroll position) were tested per viewport. Broader validation against all ~290 IOTMs is part of Phase 3.

## Phase 3 (next)

Wire entryNameHeight into the production height path:
- Replace useEntryHeights's measurement loop with pure computation: entryFixedChrome + nameTextHeight(name, vp) + tier/price section heights (which are constant-ish — no wrapping).
- Replace the virtualizer's `estimateSize` callback in List.tsx:139 to call the pure function.
- Delete MeasurementContainer rendering on initial mount (the loading-time tax we set out to eliminate).
- Keep the page-level ResizeObserver for scroll-sync; it's a separate concern.
- Add font-load gate: defer first virtualization render until `document.fonts.ready` so pretext computes against the right metrics.
- Validate against ALL ~290 entries (not just visible ones) by iterating iotms.ts data and comparing pretext heights to DOM heights for each as we scroll past them, OR by mounting a hidden audit container in dev mode.
- Remove the temporary window.__nameTextHeight exposure in entryNameHeight.ts and App.tsx import.

title: IOTM codegen: append new entries to iotms.ts
description: Build a CLI entrypoint that calls the scraper (see the scraper sub-issue) and appends any new IOTM records to packages/wishlist-shared/data/iotms.ts. Output must match the existing file's formatting so prettier is a no-op (key order matches the existing entries; trailing comma; 2-space indent; double-quoted strings). Approach options to evaluate: (a) string-templating new entries onto the file before the closing '];' marker — simple, low-risk; (b) AST manipulation via ts-morph or recast — heavier but safer if formatting drifts. Lean toward (a) for now. After write, the file should still parse and pass the existing tsconfig type-check.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Running the script with no new IOTMs is a no-op. (2) Running with a new IOTM appends the entry such that 'prettier --check' passes and 'tsc --noEmit' (via the existing tsconfig) still succeeds. (3) Idempotent: running twice in a row does not duplicate entries.
close_reason: Implemented codegen.ts (formatEntry + mergeEntries) and update.ts (CLI). 17 tests covering byte-equivalence with canonical iotms.ts blocks across all type shapes, idempotency, TODO-comment emission, and round-trip parsing.

title: IOTM scraper: fetch and parse wiki + tier list data
description: Build a TypeScript module that, given no input (or an optional 'since' anchor), returns an array of IOTM records matching IOTMSchema for any IOTMs not yet present in iotms.ts. Sources: (a) KoL wiki — start from the Items_of_the_Month index page (https://kol.coldfront.net/thekolwiki/index.php/Items_of_the_Month) and per-item pages for ids/names/type/equipment_slot/img/tradeable plus opened_*/familiar_*/skill_* depending on type; (b) Google Sheets CSV exports of the speed and aftercore tier lists (sheet IDs in wiki/GLOSSARY.md) for tier values. is_ioty and is_con remain optional/manual — leave undefined unless trivially derivable. Module returns IOTM[] only; writing to disk is a separate concern (see the codegen sub-issue). Should be runnable in isolation for testing (e.g. 'bun run scrape -- --month=2026-04' prints JSON).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Calling the scraper for a known past IOTM produces a record byte-equivalent to its existing iotms.ts entry (modulo tier values if the sheet has been updated since). (2) Calling for a 'no new IOTM' state returns []. (3) Output validates against IOTMSchema.
close_reason: Implemented at packages/wishlist-shared/scripts/update-iotms/. 42 tests, all passing. Covers 33 historical IOTMs (every type) + 4 support pages.

title: Local script to update iotms.ts from wiki + tier lists
description: Automate the manual process of updating packages/wishlist-shared/data/iotms.ts when a new IOTM is released. Scope: a locally-runnable script (no scheduled CI, no PR automation yet). Sources: KoL wiki for item metadata (ids, names, type, equipment_slot, img, tradeable); community Google Sheets tier lists (URLs in wiki/GLOSSARY.md) for speed_tier/aftercore_tier. Manual fields like is_ioty/is_con stay manual. Two pieces: (1) a scraper that produces parsed IOTM records matching IOTMSchema in packages/wishlist-shared/schemas/data.ts; (2) a codegen step that appends new entries to iotms.ts while preserving prettier formatting.
acceptance_criteria: Running the script locally detects any IOTMs released since the last entry in iotms.ts, fetches their metadata + tier values, and appends well-formatted entries that pass IOTMSchema validation and existing prettier rules.
close_reason: Both subs done. Local script ready: bun packages/wishlist-shared/scripts/update-iotms/update.ts (--dry-run for preview).
